items.q: What are the three elements of Beck's negative triad?
items.correct: Negative views of self, world, and future.
items.misconception: Negative views of past, present, and future.
items.why: Students often substitute 'past' for 'self' or 'world'. The triad is about the DOMAINS of negative thought (self/world/future), not time periods.
Students reverse IV and DV. The IV is what you CHANGE (input); the DV is what you MEASURE (outcome). Mnemonic: 'I change the I.'

items.correct: Removing an unpleasant stimulus to INCREASE a behaviour.
items.misconception: Punishing a behaviour to decrease it.
items.random_wrong: Giving a reward to decrease behaviour.
items.why: Students confuse negative reinforcement with punishment. Both involve something unpleasant, but reinforcement (positive or negative) always INCREASES behaviour; punishment decreases it.

items.q: In Ellis's REBT, what does D stand for in the extended ABCDE model?
items.why: It's Disputing. “Diagnosing” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: CBT for schizophrenia normally runs alongside which other treatment?
items.why: Correct answer: Antipsychotics. “Group therapy only” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: NICE recommends a minimum of how many CBT sessions for schizophrenia?
items.why: The real figure is 16. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
